title: Mariosousa mammifera
taxonomy: Mariosousa mammifera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Mariosousa, which is a group of plants native to the Americas. Mariosousa mammifera is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae, a large subfamily of flowering plants. The species is native to Central and South America, and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Mariosousa mammifera is a climbing legume that is characterized by its woody vines and small, fragrant flowers.

intro: Mariosousa mammifera, also known as the Mexican dogwood or Guanacaste, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central America. The health benefits of Mariosousa mammifera include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections.
